text: We present to you here a robotic partial nephrectomy for a clinical TII-A renal mass with super selective clamping. It's a sixty-one year old patient with a right upper pole renal mass suspected to be a clear cell renal cell carcinoma on a CT scan. The complexity of the nephron sparing surgery was moderate to high according to the renal nephrometry score and PADUA score as well. It was decided with the patient to proceed to a conservative surgical treatment for this tumor. After analysis of the CT scan with three D reconstructions, we planned a super selective clamping as we identified two tumor specific segmental arteries as well as a perforating upper pole renal artery. We plan to clamp these two segmental arteries right here. As we can see through this sequence here, it took us a long time to dissect into the hilum to prepare the different arterial branches and identify the ones we wanted to clamp according to the preoperative planning. The different branches are encircled with vessel loops until quaternary branches are reached.

The surgeon uses laparoscopic bulldog clamps to initially clamp the tumor-specific tertiary and quaternary segmental arteries. The gyrotus fascia is dissected completely, and the kidney is liberated from its attachments for mobilization during tumor excision and reconstruction. Bleeding interrupts the procedure, prompting a downgrade of the clamp from the quaternary to the tertiary branch. As the dissection progresses towards the back of the kidney, the perforating upper pole artery is clamped.

The surgeon progresses with the tumor excision by detaching the renal mass capsule from the normal kidney parenchyma, revealing the cleavage plane. Once the tumor is excised, the deep tumor bed is reconstructed using a III-O V-lock suture. The laparoscopic bulldog clamps are removed, and warm ischemia time is noted as thirty-three minutes. Additional III-O V-lock sutures are used to achieve hemostasis, and a Tacosil hemostatic patch is applied to manage residual bleeding from the large tumor bed.
